debate_rounds.title: Chair Initial Assessment
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 7 adds multi-language article generation and scoring calibration. The LANG_PHRASES dict provides en/zh/es/de/ja translations for all draft scaffolding phrases (introduction, disclosure, content placeholders, FAQ, references). cmd_draft now generates localized content based on config language. Scoring calibration tests verify consistency, determinism, axis-sum correctness, and keyword-sensitivity. Test coverage grew from 119 to 128 (+9). Multi-language support directly addresses the 'no multi-language article generation in cmd_draft' gap from iteration 6.

experts.rationale: Breadth: Multi-language support covers 5 languages (en/zh/es/de/ja), expanding the pipeline's reach significantly. Depth: The LANG_PHRASES dict translates all scaffolding phrases, not just labels. The scoring calibration tests verify mathematical properties of the scoring engine. Thickness: 128 tests. Language phrase tests, scoring calibration tests, and multi-language draft tests add meaningful coverage. Effectiveness: Multi-language support makes the pipeline usable for non-English markets. Scoring calibration ensures the scoring engine is reliable.

Breadth: 5-language support is a significant expansion. Scoring calibration tests add a new quality dimension. Depth: The translations cover all structural elements, making non-English drafts genuinely usable. The calibration tests verify determinism and axis-sum correctness. Thickness: 128 tests including language phrase completeness and scoring calibration. Effectiveness: Multi-language drafts open the pipeline to international markets. Scoring calibration gives confidence in the scoring engine.

Breadth: Multi-language support is a major expansion — 5 languages covers the most important SEO markets (US, China, Spanish-speaking, Germany, Japan). Scoring calibration is a new quality assurance dimension. Depth: The translations are thorough — introduction, disclosure, FAQ, references, all localized. The calibration tests verify the scoring engine's mathematical properties. Thickness: 128 tests. The scoring calibration tests are particularly valuable — they verify consistency, axis-sum, determinism, and keyword-sensitivity. Effectiveness: Multi-language drafts directly enable international SEO strategies.

Breadth: Multi-language support and scoring calibration are genuine breadth additions. Depth: The LANG_PHRASES dict covers all structural elements for 5 languages. The calibration tests verify the scoring engine's mathematical integrity. Thickness: 128 tests. Good coverage of language phrases and scoring properties. Effectiveness: Multi-language support enables international content strategies. Calibration tests ensure scoring reliability.

Breadth: Multi-language support is a real addition. But the translations are scaffold phrases only — the actual content sections remain as placeholders. Depth: The translations cover all structural elements, which is good. But a Chinese draft with English placeholder content is not truly Chinese. Thickness: 128 tests. The scoring calibration tests are valuable. Effectiveness: Multi-language scaffolding is useful for international teams, but the pipeline still needs actual content generation for each language.

challenges.challenge: Lily scores depth at 24, but the multi-language support only translates scaffolding phrases, not content. A Chinese draft still has '[Content for this section]' in Chinese brackets, which is not meaningful Chinese content. I propose reducing from 24 to 23.

Lily scores effectiveness at 23, but multi-language scaffolding without multi-language content generation is incomplete. The pipeline can produce a Chinese article outline, but not a Chinese article. I propose reducing from 23 to 22.

debate_rounds.title: Advocate Supports

supports.support: 128 tests including 5 scoring calibration tests (consistency, empty article, axis sum, determinism, keyword sensitivity), 2 language phrase tests, 2 multi-language draft tests. The calibration tests are particularly valuable — they verify mathematical properties of the scoring engine, not just that it returns values.

debate_rounds.statement: The 128 tests include calibration tests that verify the scoring engine's mathematical integrity, which is more valuable than adding more functional tests.

debate_rounds.title: Chair Synthesis
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 7 advances from 87 to 91. Multi-language support for 5 languages addresses the internationalization gap. Scoring calibration tests add a new quality assurance dimension. The project is now at 91/100 — approaching the convergence zone. Remaining gaps: (1) scaffolding vs. full content generation, (2) no actual SSG build test, (3) scoring model uses regex heuristics. These are progressively harder and represent genuine limits of what can be achieved without an LLM or real SSG integration.

gaps: Draft generates scaffolding, not full article content (requires LLM integration)
No actual SSG build test (frontmatter validation checks format, not rendering in real SSG)
Scoring model uses regex heuristics, not validated against real search performance data
Multi-language content sections are placeholders, not translated content
No semantic relevance scoring for internal link suggestions
